fr.recommendations: Ensuring that the single access point to the property is clearly marked and free from any obstructions could improve responder access and their ability to quickly reach the main building. Trimming back vegetation overhangs that encroach on driveways or pedestrian walkways may enhance the visibility and ease of movement for first responders navigating the property. Additionally, exploring ways to provide more point-of-entry options or improve paths leading to the rear sections of the property could facilitate more efficient responder movement and staging during an emergency. Maintaining open and well-defined pedestrian walkways may support effective navigation, allowing emergency personnel to carry out operations more effectively across different areas of the property, including the landscaped and open green spaces.

wildfire.recommendations: To enhance property preparedness and reduce wildfire exposure, increasing separation between vegetation and structures may be beneficial, particularly addressing the vegetation immediately adjacent to structures and along access routes. Ensuring that these areas are regularly managed would support firefighter access and suppress fire spread. Thinning tree clusters and scattered shrubs, especially where they contribute to dense canopies, could reduce fuel continuity and improve defensible space. Preserving open terrain and maintaining existing defensible space around the property should continue to be prioritized, as these open areas can serve as effective fuel breaks. Long-term resilience can be further supported by consistent vegetation management along the vegetated perimeter, thereby minimizing the risk of fire encroachment from nearby plant concentrations.
